analysis_result: FANCA mutation frequency in benign/atypical NF oncoprint: 13.9% (5/36 samples). Five unique samples with nonsilent FANCA mutations (Tumor_1467, Tumor_1682, Tumor_1874, Tumor_2300, Tumor_453) among 36 benign/atypical NF samples with WES data. If restricted to Neurofibroma (benign only, excluding Atypical_NF): 18.8% (3/16).
denominator_used: N = 36 ; definition: benign/atypical NF samples with WES data, i.e., samples in MOESM4 'Clinical Data' with Tumor Type in {Neurofibroma, Cutaneous_NF, Atypical_NF} and non-null WES column. Alternative denominator considered: N=16 if restricted to 'Neurofibroma' only (excluding Atypical_NF).
assumptions_made: Interpreted 'benign NF oncoprint' as the benign/atypical NF cohort shown in Supplementary Figure 3a per the question's approach; included Neurofibroma and Atypical_NF (0 Cutaneous_NF samples had WES); counted unique Tumor_Sample_Barcode entries with any FANCA mutation (silent and nonsilent yield same answer since the only silent variant was in Tumor_453 which also had a nonsilent missense); oncoprints typically show all mutated samples regardless of variant class; 4 samples in MOESM4 WES subset (Tumor_2178, Tumor_2138, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414) had no mutations reported in MOESM5 but are still counted in the denominator.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 samples x 15 columns; key fields: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label
- MOESM5 (Supplementary Table 2 - WES mutations): 7457 mutation rows x 123 columns; 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcode values

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Start: 108 samples in MOESM4
- Filter 1 (WES notna): 54 samples with WES data
- Filter 2 (Tumor Type in Neurofibroma/Cutaneous_NF/Atypical_NF): 36 samples
  - Atypical_NF: 20
  - Neurofibroma: 16
  - Cutaneous_NF: 0 (none had WES)
- Final analytic cohort: N = 36 benign/atypical NF samples with WES

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Tumor_Sample_Barcode constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label
- FANCA identified via Hugo_Symbol == 'FANCA'
- 6 total FANCA mutation rows across 5 unique samples: Tumor_1467 (missense p.N691S), Tumor_1682 (missense p.I525V), Tumor_1874 (missense p.L1143P), Tumor_2300 (missense p.A901V), Tumor_453 (missense p.S890N + silent p.G538=)

D. COMPUTATION
- Numerator: unique samples with FANCA mutations = 5 (all have at least one nonsilent missense)
- Denominator: 36 benign/atypical NF samples with WES
- Frequency = 5/36 = 13.9%

E. RESULT
- FANCA mutation frequency in benign/atypical NF oncoprint: 13.9% (5/36)
- Alternative (Neurofibroma only): 18.8% (3/16)
- Alternative (Atypical_NF only): 10.0% (2/20)

F. CAVEATS
- Without access to Supplementary Figure 3a directly, the exact cohort definition used in the paper's benign NF oncoprint cannot be verified
- If 'benign NF' is interpreted strictly (Neurofibroma only), frequency is 18.8%
- Four WES samples (Tumor_2178, Tumor_2138, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414) had no mutations in MOESM5; these are kept in the denominator as they underwent WES profiling
- The approach in the question explicitly says 'benign/atypical NF samples with WES' supporting the 5/36 = 13.9% answer
